title: Srikalahasti Temple
tagline: Srikalahasti Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: The Srikalahasti Temple is located in the town of Srikalahasti in Tirupati district in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India.
overview.description: The Srikalahasti Temple is located in the town of Srikalahasti in Tirupati district in the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. Siva in his aspect as Vayu is worshipped as Kalahasteeswara. The temple is also regarded as Rahu-Ketu kshetra and Dakshina Kailasam. According to regional tradition, it is said to be the site where Kannappa was ready to offer both his eyes to cover blood flowing from the Shivalinga before Shiva stopped him and granted him moksha.

topPlaces.name: Srikalahasti
topPlaces.description: Srikalahasti is a town in Tirupati district located in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is a municipality and the revenue division of Srikalahasti mandal and Srikalahasti revenue division.

topPlaces.name: Srikalahasti mandal
topPlaces.description: Srikalahasti mandal is one of the 36 mandals in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is under the administration of Srikalahasti revenue division and the headquarters are located at Srikalahasti.

topPlaces.name: Appalakunta
topPlaces.description: Appalakunta is a village in Hindupur mandal, Sri Sathya Sai district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is served by the post office at Kirekera.

topPlaces.name: Akkurthi
topPlaces.description: Akkurthi is a village in Srikalahasti mandal, located in Tirupati district of Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Muchivolu
topPlaces.description: Muchivolu is a village in Srikalahasti mandal, located in Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is situated 12 km north west of the town of Srikalahasti.

topPlaces.name: Chemuru
topPlaces.description: Chemuru is a village in Thottambedu mandal, located in Tirupati district of Indian state of Andhra Pradesh.

Srikalahasti mandal is one of the 36 mandals in Tirupati district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is under the administration of Srikalahasti revenue division and the headquarters are located at Srikalahasti.
Appalakunta is a village in Hindupur mandal, Sri Sathya Sai district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is served by the post office at Kirekera.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Srikalahasti Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Srikalahasti Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Srikalahasti Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tirupati Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (33 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Srikalahasti Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Srikalahasti Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Srikalahasti Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Srikalahasti Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Srikalahasti Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Srikalahasti, Srikalahasti mandal, Appalakunta, Akkurthi, Muchivolu.
